landmarks.name: Oz Museum
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: The Oz Museum ahead in Wamego. Follow the yellow brick road off Interstate seventy. There's no place like home.

landmarks.name: Mushroom Rock
landmarks.spoken: Off Interstate seventy ahead stand the Mushroom Rocks, giant sandstone toadstools balanced on the prairie, carved by wind and time -- once trail markers for wagons on the old Smoky Hill route.

landmarks.name: World's Largest Czech Egg
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: The world's largest Czech egg ahead in Wilson, the Czech Capital of Kansas. Twenty feet of hand-painted folk art, because every small town deserves one enormous thing.

landmarks.name: The Garden of Eden
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: The Garden of Eden ahead in Lucas -- concrete Bible scenes and populist politics in a front yard, roadside folk art like nowhere else.

landmarks.name: World's Largest Prairie Dog
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: See the world's largest prairie dog ahead near Oakley -- eight thousand pounds of concrete rodent, plus live rattlesnakes and a genuine five-legged cow. Roadside science at its very finest.

landmarks.name: Giant Buffalo Bill
landmarks.spoken: Billboard: A giant Buffalo Bill on horseback ahead near Oakley, forever charging a bronze buffalo. Bigger than life, and life on the plains was already pretty big. Please stop for real buffalos.

landmarks.name: Fick Fossil and History Museum
landmarks.spoken: Fick Fossil and History Museum ahead

landmarks.name: Monument Rocks
landmarks.spoken: South of Interstate seventy near Oakley stand Monument Rocks, chalk spires and arches left by an ancient inland sea, or was it really a sea  -- the first national natural landmark, standing alone on the high plains.
